subject: URGENT - pump shaft failure unit 3
body: Cheryl - we had a pump shaft let go on unit 3 overnight. Plant is running on the spare but we need a replacement machined ASAP. Same part as the one y'all did in February, PO 24-1187, 316SS. Can you confirm you still have the program on file and give me a turnaround time this morning? This is a plant-down priority for us. Marcus

subject: Quote request - 2" 150# flanges qty 40
body: Good morning, can I get a quote on forty 2-inch 150# raised face slip-on flanges, A105 carbon steel? Customer is asking for delivery in 3 weeks. Also do you give a break at qty 40 vs our usual 25? Thanks, Jenny

subject: Re: valve stem order - wrong surface finish?
body: Dale, the valve stems we picked up Friday are measuring rougher than spec on the sealing surface. Print calls for 16 Ra and our QC is reading 28-32 on three of the eight pieces. We need these for a job Wednesday. What can you do for us? Rick

subject: Cut tooling costs 30% - quick call this week?
body: Hi there! I'm reaching out to machine shops in the Beaumont area about our new carbide insert program. Shops like yours are saving up to 30% on tooling. Do you have 15 minutes this week for a quick call? I can also send over a free sample pack. Best, Brandon Wells, Apex Cutting Tools

subject: New customer - machining capacity question
body: Hello, we're a fabrication shop in Port Arthur and our usual machining vendor just closed their doors. We'd have steady work, mostly carbon steel weld-neck flanges and misc fittings, maybe $8-12k a month. Before we send drawings, can you tell me your typical lead times and payment terms for a new account? Sandra Okafor, Purchasing, Gulf Coast Fabrication

subject: invoice question INV-2641
body: Cheryl, separate from the pump shaft thing - our AP flagged invoice INV-2641. It shows a 35% expedite fee on the April rush job but I thought our contract waives that. Can you check? Not holding payment, just want it cleaned up. Marcus
